description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe featuring two large, symmetrical black blades with a slightly curved edge and pointed tips. The blades are mounted on a long, cylindrical wooden handle that appears textured for grip, with a dark metal or synthetic cap at the base. The axe head is robust and wide, with a central mounting collar that secures the blades to the handle. The overall design is utilitarian yet imposing, suitable for heavy-duty chopping or ceremonial display. The materials and construction suggest durability and strength, with a focus on both function and visual impact. No visible markings or decorative engravings are present on the axe.
description: A double-headed black-bladed axe with a long, textured wooden handle and dark metal end cap, designed for strength and impact.
description_view_0: Front view showing both wide, symmetrical black blades and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle's length.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and straight handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical wooden handle with visible texture and a dark metal or synthetic end cap; designed for two-handed grip.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical black blades with curved edges and pointed tips; no beard or poll, functional and robust.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with a central collar securing the blades to the handle.
